hey, could you read over these two essays? i need some feedback before i submit. theyre both kinda the same but i wanted a second opinion.

essay 1:
"Growing up in West Oakwood was… different. The streets weren’t always safe, and opportunities felt limited. Seeing friends and family struggle with systemic challenges fueled a fire in me to succeed, not just for myself, but to create change. I volunteered at the local community center, tutoring younger kids and organizing neighborhood cleanups. It showed me the power of collective action, even in the face of adversity. This experience instilled in me a deep sense of responsibility and a determination to overcome obstacles and give back to my community. I know college will be challenging, but I’m prepared to work harder than anyone else to make a difference."

essay 2:
“Growing up in Briarwood was a wonderful experience. Our neighborhood was close-knit, and there were always opportunities to get involved. While life wasn’t always easy, I was fortunate to have a supportive family and access to excellent schools. I participated in the debate club and volunteered at the local library, which fostered a love of learning and a commitment to public service. These experiences taught me the importance of intellectual curiosity and the value of contributing to something larger than myself. I am excited to continue my education at the college level and pursue my passion for knowledge.”
